html:
The Strait of Hormuz is at the center of global attention today, as a cargo ship was reportedly struck by an "unknown projectile" near Oman, prompting the UN to pause its evacuation plan for the critical waterway and driving up oil prices. Headlines from some outlets explicitly state Iran struck the vessel, raising immediate concerns over international maritime security.

US Immigration: The US Supreme Court delivered a significant victory to the Trump administration, allowing it to terminate the protected status for hundreds of thousands of Haitian and Syrian immigrants and to turn back asylum seekers at the US-Mexico border. This ruling is described as fundamentally reshaping the US asylum system.

Ukraine War: The conflict in Ukraine shows signs of escalation, with Zelensky announcing approval for a "40-day influence operation" to force Russia to end the war, including preemptive attacks on Russian sites. Concurrently, a Russian soldier in a viral video reportedly threatened Putin with an army mutiny, claiming disgruntled military and security officials asked him to convey their message.

Middle East Security: Amidst heightened tensions following the Hormuz incident, Iran has warned ships to avoid unauthorized routes, with three vessels reportedly reversing course after warnings from IRGC naval forces. US Secretary of State Marco Rubio indicated the US seeks a deal with Iran, but not "at any price," echoing Gulf foreign ministers who emphasized addressing Iran's proxies and missiles for lasting peace. Meanwhile, Israel vows to maintain military presence in Lebanon, Gaza, and Syria, stating it will only withdraw troops from Lebanon after Hezbollah is disarmed.

Global Markets: Oil prices climbed after the Strait of Hormuz attack, underscoring the waterway's critical role in global energy markets. This comes as the IMF previously reported a decline in energy and commodity prices following a memorandum of understanding with Iran. Further economic uncertainty is highlighted by Iraq's threat to exit OPEC if its quota demands are not met, with officials reportedly considering all options if quotas are not increased.

Venezuelan Earthquakes: Venezuela is grappling with the aftermath of twin seven-plus magnitude earthquakes, the largest in over a century, which have killed at least 235 people and injured thousands, with fears of many more fatalities. Rescue teams, including assistance from the US, are racing to search for survivors amid widespread destruction, adding to uncertainty six months after Nicolás Maduro was seized by US forces.

World Trends: Other significant global developments include China's Xi Jinping actively building influence by hosting numerous world leaders, as "middle powers" look beyond the US. Europe is experiencing a heatwave, prompting alcohol restrictions in Paris, while South Africa faces a looming deadline from a xenophobic group for undocumented immigrants to leave. Separately, AI technology has aided scientists in unlocking the complete text of an ancient scroll burnt by Mount Vesuvius.
